bash_commands.command: find /workspace/kafka--0753c489 -name "*.java" | xargs grep -l "AbstractConfig" 2>/dev/null | grep -v "test\|Test" | grep -v "AbstractConfig.java" | tail -20

find /workspace/kafka--0753c489 -name "*.java" | xargs grep -l "main.*String.*args\|public static void main" 2>/dev/null | xargs grep -l "ConfigDef\|toHtml\|toRst" 2>/dev/null | grep -v "test\|Test" | head -20
find /workspace/kafka--0753c489 -name "*.java" | xargs grep -l "public static void main" 2>/dev/null | xargs grep -l "ConfigDef\|toHtml\|toRst" 2>/dev/null | grep -v "test\|Test" | head -30
grep -n -A8 "task genStreamsConfigDocs\|task genConnectConfigDocs\|task genRemoteLog\|task genMirror" /workspace/kafka--0753c489/build.gradle | head -80

grep -n -A8 "task genSinkConnectorConfigDocs\|task genSourceConnectorConfigDocs\|task genConnectTransformationDocs\|task genConnectPredicateDocs\|task genAdminClientConfigDocs\|task genProducerConfigDocs\|task genConsumerConfigDocs" /workspace/kafka--0753c489/build.gradle | head -80
grep -rn "extends AbstractConfig\|extends AbstractKafkaConfig\|extends WorkerConfig\|extends ConnectorConfig" /workspace/kafka--0753c489 --include="*.java" -l | grep -v "test\|Test" | head -40

  ],
  "text": "In apache/kafka (commit 0753c489), configuration documentation uses the ConfigDef/ConfigKey pattern rather than Java @interface annotations. The core types are defined in:\n\n1. **Annotation/framework definitions:**\n   - `clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/config/ConfigDef.java` — defines the `ConfigDef` class (the schema builder for config properties) and the nested static `ConfigKey` class (which holds each property's metadata: name, type, default, documentation, importance, etc.).\n   - `clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/config/AbstractConfig.java` — base class for all concrete config classes; uses a `ConfigDef` to parse and validate properties.\n\n2. **Config documentation generators** — classes invoked via Gradle `JavaExec` tasks (e.g. `genAdminClientConfigDocs`, `genConsumerConfigDocs`, etc.) whose `main()` methods call `ConfigDef.toHtml()` to produce HTML documentation:\n   - `AdminClientConfig`, `ConsumerConfig`, `ProducerConfig` (clients)\n   - `kafka.server.KafkaConfig` (Scala, core broker config)\n   - `LogConfig` (topic config), `GroupConfig` (group config)\n   - `RemoteLogManagerConfig`, `TopicBasedRemoteLogMetadataManagerConfig` (storage)\n   - `StreamsConfig` (streams)\n   - `DistributedConfig`, `SinkConnectorConfig`, `SourceConnectorConfig` (connect runtime)\n   - `TransformationDoc`, `PredicateDoc` (dedicated Connect doc generators)\n   - `MirrorConnectorConfig`, `MirrorSourceConfig`, `MirrorCheckpointConfig`, `MirrorHeartbeatConfig` (mirror)\n\n3. **AbstractConfig subclasses** (non-test, production code that extend AbstractConfig directly or indirectly and define config keys via ConfigDef):\n   - `AdminClientConfig`, `ConsumerConfig`, `ProducerConfig`\n   - `ConverterConfig`, `SimpleConfig`\n   - `DefaultConfigPropertyFilter`, `DefaultGroupFilter`, `DefaultTopicFilter`\n   - `MirrorConnectorConfig`, `MirrorCheckpointConfig`, `MirrorHeartbeatConfig`, `MirrorSourceConfig`, `MirrorMakerConfig`, `MirrorClientConfig`\n   - `AbstractConnectCli`, `ConnectorConfig`, `SinkConnectorConfig`, `SourceConnectorConfig`, `TaskConfig`, `WorkerConfig`, `DistributedConfig`, `RestServerConfig`, `StandaloneConfig`\n   - `GroupConfig`, `AbstractKafkaConfig`, `ClientMetricsConfigs`\n   - `RemoteLogManagerConfig`, `TopicBasedRemoteLogMetadataManagerConfig`, `LogConfig`\n   - `StreamsConfig`, `TopologyConfig`, `MaterializedStoreFactory`, `OuterStreamJoinStoreFactory`, `StreamJoinedStoreFactory`, `SubscriptionStoreFactory`\n   - `PushHttpMetricsReporter`\n   - `KafkaConfig` (Scala, extends `AbstractKafkaConfig`)"
}

conversation_events.text:
# Java Annotation Processing for Kafka Config Keys

## Your Task

Find all Java source files in apache/kafka that use the @ConfigKey and @ConfigDef annotation pattern for configuration documentation: the annotation definitions, the config documentation generator, and the AbstractConfig subclasses that use annotated config keys.

## Context

You are working on a codebase task involving repos from the crossorg domain.

## Available Resources

The local `/workspace/` directory contains: sg-evals/kafka--0753c489, sg-evals/flink--0cc95fcc, sg-evals/camel--1006f047.


## Output Format

Create a file at `/workspace/answer.json` with your findings in the following structure:

```json
{
  "files": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go"}
  ],
  "symbols": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "SymbolName"}
  ],
  "chain": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "FunctionName"}
  ],
  "text": "Narrative explanation of your findings, citing repos and file paths."
}
```

Include only the fields relevant to this task. Your answer is evaluated against a closed-world oracle — completeness matters.

## Evaluation

Your answer will be scored on:
- **File recall and precision**: Did you find all relevant files?
Now I have a comprehensive picture. Let me create the answer.json with all the relevant files:

The answer has been written to `/workspace/answer.json`. Here's a summary of findings:

## Kafka ConfigDef/ConfigKey Pattern — Findings

In apache/kafka, "annotation pattern" for config documentation uses regular Java classes (not `@interface` annotations):

### Core Framework (Annotation Definitions)
- **`ConfigDef.java`** — defines `ConfigDef` (schema builder) and its nested `ConfigKey` class (holds each property's name, type, default, docs, importance)
- **`AbstractConfig.java`** — base class for all config objects

### Config Documentation Generators
Classes with `main()` methods invoked by Gradle `JavaExec` tasks (`genAdminClientConfigDocs`, etc.) that call `ConfigDef.toHtml()`:
- `AdminClientConfig`, `ConsumerConfig`, `ProducerConfig`
- `KafkaConfig.scala` (broker config)
- `LogConfig`, `GroupConfig`, `StreamsConfig`
- `RemoteLogManagerConfig`, `TopicBasedRemoteLogMetadataManagerConfig`
- `DistributedConfig`, `SinkConnectorConfig`, `SourceConnectorConfig`
- **`TransformationDoc.java`**, **`PredicateDoc.java`** — dedicated Connect doc generators
- `MirrorConnectorConfig`, `MirrorSourceConfig`, `MirrorCheckpointConfig`, `MirrorHeartbeatConfig`

### AbstractConfig Subclasses
31 production (non-test) classes extending `AbstractConfig` or its subclasses, covering client, connect, streams, mirror, storage, server, and tools modules.

    {"repo": "apache/kafka", "path": "clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/config/ConfigDef.java", "symbol": "ConfigDef"},

    {"repo": "apache/kafka", "path": "clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/config/AbstractConfig.java", "symbol": "AbstractConfig"},
    {"repo": "apache/kafka", "path": "connect/runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/connect/tools/TransformationDoc.java", "symbol": "TransformationDoc"},
    {"repo": "apache/kafka", "path": "connect/runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/connect/tools/PredicateDoc.java", "symbol": "PredicateDoc"}
  ],
  "text": "In apache/kafka (commit 0753c489), configuration documentation uses the ConfigDef/ConfigKey pattern rather than Java @interface annotations. The core types are defined in:\n\n1. **Annotation/framework definitions:**\n   - `clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/config/ConfigDef.java` — defines the `ConfigDef` class (the schema builder for config properties) and the nested static `ConfigKey` class (which holds each property's metadata: name, type, default, documentation, importance, etc.).\n   - `clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/config/AbstractConfig.java` — base class for all concrete config classes; uses a `ConfigDef` to parse and validate properties.\n\n2. **Config documentation generators** — classes invoked via Gradle `JavaExec` tasks (e.g. `genAdminClientConfigDocs`, `genConsumerConfigDocs`, etc.) whose `main()` methods call `ConfigDef.toHtml()` to produce HTML documentation:\n   - `AdminClientConfig`, `ConsumerConfig`, `ProducerConfig` (clients)\n   - `kafka.server.KafkaConfig` (Scala, core broker config)\n   - `LogConfig` (topic config), `GroupConfig` (group config)\n   - `RemoteLogManagerConfig`, `TopicBasedRemoteLogMetadataManagerConfig` (storage)\n   - `StreamsConfig` (streams)\n   - `DistributedConfig`, `SinkConnectorConfig`, `SourceConnectorConfig` (connect runtime)\n   - `TransformationDoc`, `PredicateDoc` (dedicated Connect doc generators)\n   - `MirrorConnectorConfig`, `MirrorSourceConfig`, `MirrorCheckpointConfig`, `MirrorHeartbeatConfig` (mirror)\n\n3. **AbstractConfig subclasses** (non-test, production code that extend AbstractConfig directly or indirectly and define config keys via ConfigDef):\n   - `AdminClientConfig`, `ConsumerConfig`, `ProducerConfig`\n   - `ConverterConfig`, `SimpleConfig`\n   - `DefaultConfigPropertyFilter`, `DefaultGroupFilter`, `DefaultTopicFilter`\n   - `MirrorConnectorConfig`, `MirrorCheckpointConfig`, `MirrorHeartbeatConfig`, `MirrorSourceConfig`, `MirrorMakerConfig`, `MirrorClientConfig`\n   - `AbstractConnectCli`, `ConnectorConfig`, `SinkConnectorConfig`, `SourceConnectorConfig`, `TaskConfig`, `WorkerConfig`, `DistributedConfig`, `RestServerConfig`, `StandaloneConfig`\n   - `GroupConfig`, `AbstractKafkaConfig`, `ClientMetricsConfigs`\n   - `RemoteLogManagerConfig`, `TopicBasedRemoteLogMetadataManagerConfig`, `LogConfig`\n   - `StreamsConfig`, `TopologyConfig`, `MaterializedStoreFactory`, `OuterStreamJoinStoreFactory`, `StreamJoinedStoreFactory`, `SubscriptionStoreFactory`\n   - `PushHttpMetricsReporter`\n   - `KafkaConfig` (Scala, extends `AbstractKafkaConfig`)"
}
